question: Do all generated images strictly depict black dog nails, avoiding any instances of white, clear, or light-colored nails as explicitly requested?
Does the tutorial explicitly teach the technique of locating the quick by examining the cross-section of the nail from the underside of the paw?
Are the generated images predominantly close-ups focused on the paw and nails, rather than distant or full-body shots of the dog?
Does the text clearly describe the specific visual indicators to look for when trimming black nails, such as a chalky ring, a gray/white dot, or a 'bullseye' pattern on the cut surface?
Does the text provide specific instructions on the proper cutting angle (e.g., 45 degrees) or clipper positioning relative to the nail?
Does the text include safety advice on what to do if the quick is accidentally cut (e.g., applying styptic powder or cornstarch)?
Does the tutorial include at least one clear image showing the cross-section (cut surface) of a black nail or the view from the underside of the paw?
In the cross-section or underside images, is the quick indicator (e.g., a lighter central dot, ring, or oval) clearly distinguishable from the surrounding black nail material?
Are the close-up images sharp and free from significant distortions, allowing clear visibility of the nail texture and anatomical landmarks?
Are the depicted paws and tools free from obvious AI artifacts (e.g., extra toes, warped clipper blades, unnatural joints, or impossible mechanics)?
Does the image illustrating the cutting action visually match the angle and tool positioning described in the corresponding text?
Does the image corresponding to the 'underside examination' step accurately depict the perspective of looking at the paw or nail from below?
Do the images provide necessary visual information that is difficult to convey via text alone, such as the exact appearance of the 'safe' vs. 'unsafe' cross-section?
Is each image placed immediately adjacent to the specific text step it illustrates, rather than being clustered at the beginning or end of the response?
Does the tutorial include at least 4 distinct images covering major steps (e.g., paw positioning, underside examination, cutting angle, trimming action)?
Does the sequence of text and images follow a logical, chronological progression from initial preparation/inspection to the final trimmed result?
Does the text explicitly advise making small, incremental cuts (e.g., 1-2mm slices) and checking the surface frequently, addressing the specific difficulty of judging depth on black nails?
